summary: Fixed all three tool display bugs. BUG 1 (spinner hangs): the tool_result handler in use-rust-agent.ts was mutating ToolCall objects in-place (first.status = ...) then returning the same object references inside a new array. SolidJS For reconciles by object identity, so it kept existing component instances without re-rendering. Fix: create a new object via spread (updated[firstIdx] = { ...first, status: ..., output: ..., completedAt: ..., diff: ... }) so SolidJS detects a changed element and re-renders the ToolIcon/ToolCallCard components immediately. BUG 2 (context grouping): the ToolSegmentDispatch in MessageBubble.tsx correctly groups context tools via partitionByContext for completed messages, but LiveStreamingBlock.tsx rendered all tool calls as individual ToolCallCard instances during live streaming, bypassing the grouping logic entirely. Fix: import partitionByContext and ContextGroupHeader into LiveStreamingBlock, then apply partitionByContext to all three tool rendering sites (completed fallback path, interleaved segment tools, orphan tool calls). BUG 3 (duplicate timestamp): TimestampLine was always rendered for assistant messages, even during isActiveStreaming(). While the timestamp text was internally guarded by Show when={!props.isStreaming}, the outer h-[20px] container and MessageActions absolute overlay were still present in the DOM during streaming, creating a visible empty gap and potential duplicate layout artifact at the bottom of streaming messages. Fix: wrap TimestampLine in Show when={!isActiveStreaming()} so it is only mounted after the agent completes.

bug1.root_cause: In-place mutation of ToolCall objects in the tool_result handler. SolidJS For uses object identity for reconciliation, so mutating a field on an existing object does not trigger component re-render.
bug1.fix: Replaced setActiveToolCalls mutation pattern with updated[firstIdx] = { ...first, status, output, completedAt, diff } to produce a new object reference at that index.

bug2.root_cause: LiveStreamingBlock rendered tool calls as raw ToolCallCard instances without going through partitionByContext. The grouping logic existed in ToolSegmentDispatch (used by MessageBubble for completed messages) but was not applied during live streaming.
bug2.fix: Imported partitionByContext and ContextGroupHeader. Applied partitionByContext to completed tool calls in the fallback path, to segment tools in InterleavedSegment, and to orphan tool calls. Context groups with >1 call render as ContextGroupHeader; single calls render as ToolCallCard.

bug3.root_cause: TimestampLine was unconditionally mounted for all assistant messages. During isActiveStreaming(), the timestamp text was internally hidden by Show when={!props.isStreaming}, but the outer container (h-[20px] div) and the absolute MessageActions overlay were still present in the DOM, adding an empty 20px spacer at the bottom of every streaming message and potentially showing the actions overlay.
bug3.fix: Wrapped TimestampLine in Show when={!isActiveStreaming()} so it is only mounted for completed assistant messages.
